title: Add HAL GNSS/location/time snapshots for app compatibility
description: Why this exists: Meshtastic node_info/status can include position and time, but the current Zephyr HAL only exposes GNSS device presence and no fix/time snapshot. What needs to be done: define a board-agnostic location/time provider API with validity flags for lat/lon/alt, fix source, satellites, fix time, and uptime-only fallback policy; feed native LICHEN status and Meshtastic Position from it. Acceptance criteria: unknown position/time is omitted or explicitly marked placeholder; no uptime is encoded as GPS epoch time; tests cover absent and synthetic providers.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware HAL/app/Meshtastic location-time snapshot slice. Added lichen_hal_location_time_snapshot_get() with validity flags for lat/lon/alt/fix_time/satellites/fix_source and CONFIG_ZTEST injection; uptime-only provider does not set time_provider_available and never becomes Unix/GPS time. Added app status location_time fields. Meshtastic NodeInfo now emits Position only when lat+lon are valid, with optional altitude, time/timestamp, location/altitude source, and satellites; absent/unknown fields are omitted. Gateway adapter maps HAL snapshots into local NodeInfo; gateway /status CBOR includes provider flags and optional location/time fields. Created follow-ups project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.3.1 (physical GNSS validation), .6.3.2 (gateway status CBOR unit coverage), and .6.3.3 (time-only Position policy decision).
Review evidence: initial codereview Hypatia found uptime-only time_provider_available incorrectly implied absolute time; fixed by requiring LICHEN_HAL_TIME_GNSS before setting time_provider_available. Initial codereview Dewey found negative altitude protobuf int32 varint lacked coverage; fixed with payload_get_varint64_field() and test_node_info_encodes_negative_altitude_as_int32_varint. Dewey also raised time-only Position policy; preserved current lat+lon requirement and filed decision follow-up project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.3.3. Final codereview Confucius: no findings, git diff --check passed. Final codereview Singer: no findings; noted local Twister unavailable due missing pykwalify, while EC2 Twister covered native_sim acceptance.
Validation evidence: local git diff --check PASS. Local python/.venv/bin/python -m pytest python/tests/test_vectors.py -q PASS: 81 passed in 0.28s. EC2 builder i-05e27e074db786e22 using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn65-validate: focused native_sim PASS after fixes: hal 8 pass/1 skip; app_interface 13 pass; meshtastic_codec 32 pass including negative altitude; meshtastic_gateway_adapter 17 pass. Gateway native_sim build PASS; size build/gateway-native-t2hn63-fixed/zephyr/zephyr.elf text=150479 data=6001 bss=47933. Final Twister native_sim command: west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-t2hn63-fixed-native --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn65-validate/lichen. Result PASS: 7 scenarios selected, 6/7 configurations passed, 1 static skip, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 134 test cases executed, 11 skipped.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware HAL location/time snapshot API, app status propagation, Meshtastic Position emission, gateway mapping/status exposure, synthetic and absent-provider tests, EC2 native_sim/Twister validation, and codereview fixes. Hardware GNSS validation and remaining policy/test gaps filed as child Beads.
